metadata.description: Lean, optional overlay to tailor CV extraction and phrasing for QA Engineers without bloating core frameworks.

senior_indicators: Architected test strategy and standards across teams
Increased automation coverage and stability (e.g., flaky tests < 2%)
Reduced defect escape rate and accelerated release cadence
Owned quality gates and release readiness/rollback criteria
Mentored QA engineers; improved team throughput/first-pass quality
Led compliance/audit readiness with zero critical findings
impact_metrics: Audit results (findings → zero) and compliance maintained
achievement_templates: Designed [tool/language]-based automation framework ([N]+ cases), reducing manual effort from [X]h to [Y]h per cycle; enabled [frequency] releases (↑[K]x)
Reduced defect escape rate from [X]% to [Y]% by implementing [strategy/process] across [N] modules; prevented [#] critical incidents
Led PCI-DSS/GDPR testing program across [system]; achieved zero critical findings over [#] audits; maintained [uptime]% SLA
Introduced data integrity validation suite (SQL) covering [N] data fields; prevented [#] payroll/calculation errors affecting [user_count] users
Drove flakiness down to [Y]% by stabilizing pipeline tests; improved CI lead time from [X]m to [Y]m; reduced change failure rate to [Z]%
missing_info_prompts: How many test cases (total and automated)? Coverage %?
What was defect escape rate before/after? Over how many releases?
What release cadence did you support before/after?
Any compliance frameworks (PCI-DSS/ISO/GDPR) with audit outcomes?
What user/client/transaction scale did the system handle?
What was the measurable impact of performance testing (p95 latency, throughput)?

scoring_overrides.notes: Slightly higher emphasis on measurable quality impact and automation outcomes.

brevity.rule: Lead with metric; 1 result per bullet; avoid repeating responsibilities.
